text:
Once upon a moonlit night, there lived a frog named Frobius. Frobius resided in a cozy burrow beside a tranquil forest lake. Every day, Frobius would hop gracefully from his burrow to the pond, swimming across its calm waters with a bounce in his hoppy steps.

Frobius had a peculiar hobby—each night before bedtime, he would whisper sweet tales from his dreams to imaginary friends. They listened intently and giggled at Frobius's silly imaginative adventures.

One tale Frobius spun involved a wise old owl with a melodious voice. Every night, in this tale, the owl would teach Frobius the secrets of starlight navigation. Frobius practiced saying softly, "Follow me, stars!"

Every morning, Frobius would observe the way starlight guided his jump strokes underwater. He became quite adept at star hopping across the night sky.

However, life had a funny way of surprising even the most skilled frogs
